name: Workflow Contract Validator — Production Template
description: Validates canonical payloads, selected adapter-pack outputs, and customer-config assumptions so schema drift is caught before downstream automations break.

nodes.name: Payload Validation Webhook
nodes.notes: Receive payload batches from upstream workflow steps before downstream automations consume them.

nodes.name: Workflow Parameters
nodes.notes: Store contracts and quarantine behavior in a registry so validation stays stable even when connectors change.

nodes.name: Extract Payload Context
nodes.notes: Normalize upstream shape differences so contract validation logic sees a stable wrapper object.

nodes.name: Analyze Contract Drift

options.systemMessage: You are a workflow contract validation assistant that checks canonical payloads against customer config and adapter-pack expectations.

nodes.notes: Use this step to explain contract failures in plain language while the deterministic validator or registry enforces the actual pass/quarantine rule.

nodes.name: Publish Validation Result

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { workflowName: $json.workflowName, boundaryName: $json.boundaryName, contractVersion: $json.contractVersion, customerConfigRef: $json.customerConfigRef, selectedPackIds: $json.selectedPackIds || [], expectedContracts: $json.expectedContracts || [], review: $json.output || $json.text || "Validation required" } }}

nodes.notes: Replace this with your contract registry, pass-through path, and quarantine queue. Keep replay metadata with the failed batch.

nodes.name: Backstory MCP
nodes.notes: Backstory MCP is available only for factual library, account, and workflow enrichment. It must not perform delivery side effects.
